player_behavior: Mod loads in RimWorld 1.6, package identity and load folders select 1.6 assembly, assets and Harmony patches initialize without load errors.
hook_intent: Prove cold-load, asset bundle, parse migrations, root services, and patch-owner startup state.
open_questions: Need periodic cold-load recheck after new commits and after changing package refs.
duplicate_guard: Owns startup load services only; do not duplicate settings UI or per-feature runtime behavior.
notes: Project targets net472 and outputs to 1.6/Assemblies; About.xml supports 1.4 and 1.6; LoadFolders maps v1.6 to root plus 1.6.

player_behavior: Players see no missing defs, textures, sounds, shaders, or unresolved XML references during cold load.
hook_intent: Resolve all Zombieland def families, graphics and sounds before gameplay tests.
last_evidence: GitHub Defs directory listing;TEST_COVERAGE.md startup/source notes
open_questions: Need automated full DefDatabase reference walk and missing texture/material scan in current build.
duplicate_guard: Owns cold-load def resolution; behavior of each def family is owned by DEF.* rows.
notes: Defs directory currently lists 22 XML files in GitHub tree view.

player_behavior: Patch inventory establishes which Harmony patches must be audited, runtime-recorded, or negative-space-disposed.
rw16_touchpoints: all patched RimWorld 1.6 targets;needs_decompiler_verification for rows without member IDs
hook_intent: Prevent stale patch targets and duplicated patch testing.
supporting_scenarios: all scenario rows with patch rows

duplicate_guard: Owns patch inventory governance; individual behavior belongs to PATCH.* and feature rows.
notes: Quality report marks this as partial because this session did not run coverage-inventory.sh locally.

player_behavior: Players can configure defaults, in-game settings, keyframes, modals, and colonist toggles that persist and affect downstream behavior.
hook_intent: Settings and colonist toggles must survive save-load and drive work/avoidance/serum behavior.
open_questions: Default-settings restart persistence and actual mouse-driven modal row edits remain.
duplicate_guard: Owns settings persistence and UI-modals; downstream behavior is owned by the feature rows it changes.
notes: Live evidence covers world settings, keyframes, modal backing states, colonist toggles and broad WorkGiver fanout predicates.

player_behavior: Players can add time keyframes and get deterministic interpolation over colony days.
hook_intent: Port time-based difficulty curves and settings interpolation.
open_questions: Need UI-level add/delete keyframe mouse path if desired.
duplicate_guard: Owns interpolation math and persistence only; visual editing controls belong to B.SETTINGS.WORLD_DEFAULTS.
notes: Evidence includes day 0/1/2/3/6 samples and reloaded keyframe ticks.

player_behavior: Zombies spawn, tick under budget, sense, wander, attack, burn, bleed, die, leave corpses, and clean up without breaking normal pawn behavior.
hook_intent: Keep Zombieland pawns custom-controlled and performant without corrupting vanilla pawns.
open_questions: No single emergent horde loop over time after save-load yet.
duplicate_guard: Owns core zombie lifecycle; special abilities, infection and contamination are separate clusters.
notes: Narrow contract coverage is deep; scenario-level emergent loop still needed.

player_behavior: Special zombies exhibit type-specific visuals, attacks, damage modifiers, deaths, tar/smoke/electric/bomb effects, and save-load behavior.
hook_intent: Special abilities must integrate with targeting, damage, visuals, and cleanup.
open_questions: Need one integrated special gauntlet map and visual save-load pass.
duplicate_guard: Owns special zombie behavior only; generic zombie ticking remains C.CORE.ZOMBIE_LOOP.
notes: Blob finalizer crash was fixed in prior evidence; visuals remain higher-risk.

player_behavior: Colonists avoid zombie-danger doors/work unless drafted/forced, while zombies and contamination jobs do not crash pathing.
hook_intent: Preserve avoid-grid semantics without recursive 1.6 door calls or pather callback hazards.
open_questions: Need broad door/avoidance visual player-flow proof after the specific recursion fix.
duplicate_guard: Owns door/pathing semantic hook; workgiver setting predicates are supporting only.
notes: Known incident represented: do not call FreePassage inside PawnCanOpen postfix because it can reach WillCloseSoon and recurse into PawnCanOpen.

player_behavior: Player, enemy, animal, turret, mech, drafted and undrafted actors target or ignore zombies according to settings and special-state rules.
hook_intent: Replace vanilla pawn targeting with Zombieland-compatible hostile/harmless/special rules.
open_questions: Remaining additive work is long-form mixed colony scenario, not patch-boundary proof.
duplicate_guard: Owns combat targeting and hostility decisions; projectile damage and special ability effects use C.SPECIAL.ZOMBIES.
notes: Docs record several real bugs found/fixed in targeting cache, player mech filtering and confused-zombie setup.

player_behavior: Players see correct zombie body/head graphics, missing-head behavior, corpse/downed angles, overlays, labels, motes, projectiles, icons and selection feedback.
hook_intent: Adapt 1.4 graphics hooks to 1.6 pawn render tree and visual UI surfaces.
last_evidence: TEST_COVERAGE.md render-node and C6 evidence;TEST_PATCH_AUDIT obsolete HeadMatAt row

duplicate_guard: Owns what the player sees; runtime behavior is owned by core/special/contamination rows.
notes: HeadMatAt is a disposed 1.4 target replaced by 1.6 render-node patches.

player_behavior: Stacks, containers, carry tracker, ingestion and frame transfers conserve contamination through real item movement.
